Article Title: Three Michigan brothers sold lemonade for 50 cents, then were told they needed nearly $400 in permits. They fought back and won
Article Text:
Representational AI photo

Poll Vote & Share your view Should kids be encouraged to run their own businesses like lemonade stands? Yes, it teaches valuable skills! No, it's too much responsibility for kids. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

When life gives you lemons, you make lemonades, and you don't let anyone stop you from selling it and making a fortune. The brothers Ethan (13), Seth (11), and Jonathan (8) had been running their shop Triple M Goods, sellling lemonade at a local farmers' market for years. The family went in shock when local officials told them they needed a permit every two weeks which would cost $57.With Michigan's warm summer season, runnning from June through August, lemonade stands are a familiar sight as children look to earn a little extra pocket money during their school break. Selling lemonade for just 50 cents a cup, the boys would have needed to sell hundreds of cups just to recover the permit fees.The boys didn't have the money, but they had the courage and perseverance- important lesson they teach you at B schools. The little brothers with the help of their mother, reached out to state Rep. Cam Cavitt leading to a bill to end permit requirements for minors who sell lemonade and earn less than $5000 a year.The Mielke family also testified before Michigan's House Regulatory Reform Committee in support of the bill.It passed the Michigan House last week and has now moved to the Senate for consideration."I told him about the fees my brothers would have to pay and gave him letters we wrote. He said that he would help us figure out how to change that law. We hope this bill can come to a vote and pass so that other kids like us do not have to pay fees to sell lemonade," 13-year-old Ethan said, as quoted by GOP, Michigan.When the news of success reached the kids, they were excited, their mother Jessica told The Washington Post. "It was a pretty neat experience for them to see how the process works when you have an idea or want a law changed."What was more interesting was how both Republicans and Democrats came together to pass the bill unanimously.For generations, lemonade stands have been an American childhood tradition as a first lesson in earning money, dealing with customers and learning the basics of business. According to New York Pots, some US states allow these stands to work without permits.
Article Summary: Brothers Ethan (13), Seth (11), and Jonathan (8) had been running their shop Triple M Goods, sellling lemonade at a local farmers' market for years. The family went in shock when local officials told them they needed a permit every two weeks which would cost $57. Selling lemonade for just 50 cents a cup, the boys would have needed to sell hundreds of cups just to recover the permit fees.

Article Title: 'Turn off fake news': 'Dr. Trump' prescribes cure for 'TDS' in new AI video
Article Text:
New AI video posted by US President Donald Trump

US President Donald Trump on Friday shared an AI-generated video portraying himself as a doctor prescribing a tongue-in-cheek "treatment" for what he calls "Trump Derangement Syndrome" (TDS).In the 90-second AI-generated clip shared on Truth Social, Trump appears dressed in a white doctor's coat, introduces himself as "Dr. Trump", and presents a satirical cure for the supposed condition."Have you or someone you know been diagnosed with TDS? The symptoms can be relentless. Fortunately, I'm Dr. Trump, and I have a treatment plan. Let's hear what some of my patients have to say," Trump says in the clip."The treatment is simple. Turn off fake news. Say your prayers. And if you ever feel anxious, just have a Diet Coke like me, and you're going to see a remarkable difference in your life," he adds.The video also features AI-generated versions of several well-known figures who have publicly criticised Trump and his policies.These include television personalities Whoopi Goldberg and Rosie O'Donnell, along with actors Robert De Niro, Julia Roberts, Edward Norton and John Leguizamo, all seemingly endorsing the fictional treatment.One of the "patients" says: "I have been suffering for over a decade. And after listening to Dr. Trump, I can see some results. Man, I've been suffering for years."This latest post adds to a growing series of AI-generated images and videos shared by Trump over the past few months.Several of these posts have sparked widespread discussion, including a manipulated image depicting the US president alongside a holy figure during his public disagreement with Pope Leo XIV.Following criticism, that particular image was removed from the platform.However, Trump later shared another AI-generated image showing himself embracing the spiritual leader in front of the Star-Spangled Banner.
Article Summary: US President Donald Trump on Friday shared an AI-generated video portraying himself as a doctor prescribing a tongue-in-cheek "treatment" for what he calls "Trump Derangement Syndrome" In the 90-second clip, Trump appears dressed in a white doctor's coat, introduces himself as "Dr. Trump", and presents a satirical cure for the supposed condition.

Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Points to keep in mind

Update your will regularly: You can keep your will up-to-date by regularly updating it on the platform to reflect any changes in your life or financial situation.

Communicate with family: It's also important to store your will safely and let your family know where to find it when needed.

Creating a will online: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. Here's how to make an online will.Begin by choosing a reliable online service. Look for a platform that has a simple interface, comprehensive templates, and secure storage for your documents.To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.Next, make a list of all your assets. Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: ToneTag eKosha to convert UPI soundboxes into banking touch points
Article Text:
ToneTag eKosha to convert UPI soundboxes into banking touch points

MUMBAI: Bengaluru-based fintech ToneTag has launched a tech stack that allows shopkeepers to use their UPI soundboxes for conversational banking without requiring the tech savviness needed for mobile or internet banking. ToneTag’s speciality is enabling payments using sound-based contactless data transfer without a network, the latest offering builds on its proprietary technology.The product, branded eKosha, converts existing payment acceptance devices into always-on banking touchpoints powered by ToneTag’s proprietary analog Edge AI, allowing merchants to access services such as credit, overdrafts, cheque book requests and scheme information through natural language voice commands.Speaking to TOI, founder and CEO Kumar Abhishek said the company has embedded its core protocol directly into soundboxes already deployed across the country. “We packed this protocol into the sound boxes which you see in the ecosystem. And now we have like more than 3 million touch points covering 73% of India's pin code already there in the ecosystem. We are doing roughly close to 28 million daily interactions on these.And on a value term, we almost $4 billion worth of transactions flow through this every month,” he said.Kumar framed the launch against the broader need to improve MSME productivity as India scales up economically. “If you look from a macro perspective, we are talking about India moving from $4 trillion to $10 trillion. And today also 30%, close to 40% of contribution to the GDP comes from the work which the MSME does… which means they need much more quicker access to working capital, at a much more lower cost,” he said.At the core of eKosha is a low-cost edge-processing architecture that enables conversational AI on devices that typically cost about Rs 1,000. “By just adding another 350 to 500 rupees to your nominal soundbox cost… we are able to provide conversational access to our MSMEs in a language which they understand,” Kumar said, adding that the system pre-processes speech locally using the company’s IP before sending it for further computation. ToneTag said it holds 21 global patents around its sound-based data transfer and conversational processing stack.The company is betting on the scale advantage of soundboxes over traditional POS terminals. “POS machines cost you north of 7,000 rupees… roughly three to three and a half million from your 60 million MSME network can pay for it. But sound boxes… have already scaled north of 16–17 million now,” Kumar said, positioning affordability as the key driver of mass adoption.He likened the evolution of soundboxes to the role ATMs played in retail banking. “These boxes are very similar to what ATM did for consumers… these boxes are now going to become a branch banking channel for India's MSME,” he said. “Now a merchant… with the press of a button, can talk to his bank and enquire for a credit, can enquire for an overdraft, can request for a checkbook.”ToneTag said the platform enables banks to build continuous engagement with merchants, turning each transaction into a potential interaction point. It supports UPI, UPI 123Pay and CBDC flows, while layering banking services such as loans, support and government scheme discovery via voice, without requiring app downloads or digital literacy.For banks, the company claims the model can reduce merchant servicing costs by up to 60%, double cross-sell opportunities and cut churn significantly by enabling direct, data-rich relationships without relying on intermediaries.
Article Summary: Bengaluru-based fintech ToneTag has launched a tech stack that allows shopkeepers to use UPI soundboxes for conversational banking. ToneTag’s speciality is enabling payments using sound-based contactless data transfer without a network. The product, branded eKosha, converts existing payment acceptance devices into always-on banking touchpoints.

Article Title: Recruiters deploy AI to catch ghost coders
Article Text: BENGALURU: The rise of GenAI is exposing new vulnerabilities in campus hiring, as recruiters confront increasingly sophisticated cheating techniques-from hidden wearable devices and AI-assisted tools to proxy candidates and ghost coders taking control of assessments via remote desktop software.Recruiters say organised "interview-as-a-service" networks are making hiring fraud more sophisticated, prompting companies to deploy AI-powered proctoring as a key line of defence. Many Indian tech firms use AI-powered proctoring platforms like Talview and Mercer Mettl and assessment platforms like Hackerearth and Hackerrank to safeguard the integrity of hiring assessments."We flag about 30%-35% of sessions with at least one suspicious behaviour. The biggest issue is the use of AI-powered cheating apps," Vivek Ravisankar, co-founder and CEO of tech hiring platform HackerRank told TOI.To combat this, companies are moving away from browser-based assessments. AI-powered proctoring analyses webcam feeds, audio and screen activity to detect suspicious behaviour and safeguard the integrity of large-scale assessments.HackerEarth says cheating methods have evolved just as rapidly.According to its CEO, Vikas Aditya, the four most common forms of malpractice today are AI-generated code submissions, proxy candidates hired through Discord or Telegram groups, off-camera assistance through secondary devices or another individual feeding answers, and virtual machines or remote-desktop software that conceal a second AI session from proctoring systems."The common thread is that nearly all of these exploit the same weakness-a static assessment that scores only the final answer without observing how it was produced," Aditya said. "Our most effective defence is a short live follow-up interview where candidates explain their solution. Most candidates who relied on AI fail within two questions."HackerEarth's 2025 Technical Hiring Landscape Report shows the share of companies using proctored technical assessments rose from 64% at the beginning of 2025 to a peak of 77% by July.Across the year, nearly two-thirds of all technical assessments were proctored, reflecting growing concern over AI-enabled cheating. The industry believes many companies still underestimate the scale of the problem because conventional coding tests reveal only the final score-not how it was achieved.
Article Summary: Recruiters say organised "interview-as-a-service" networks are making hiring fraud more sophisticated. Many Indian tech firms use AI-powered proctoring platforms like Talview and Mercer Mettl to safeguard the integrity of hiring assessments.

Article Title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro data leak: Tata Electronics breach exposes key components — What we know
Article Text:
What has leaked:

An alleged ransomware attack on Tata Electronics has left Apple’s sensitive supply chain data exposed on the dark web. The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack.The exposure could impact Apple’s tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones. It may also put pressure on Apple’s relationship with Tata, which has emerged as a key manufacturing partner outside China. The leak could additionally offer rivals, counterfeiters and vendors rare insight into Apple’s supplier network.Tata Electronics, which both supplies parts and assembles iPhones, is a key part of Apple’s push to expand manufacturing outside China. This move also fits into India’s plan, supported by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, to grow into a major electronics manufacturing hub.Apple is expected to launch its iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max in September. The leak comes as the company faces higher costs, after recently raising prices of iPads and MacBooks due to rising chip costs.Analysts expect iPhone prices may also increase in the coming months.Reuters reported that more than 200,000 files from Tata Electronics were posted on the dark web by a group called World Leaks. Those files reportedly included design documents for older iPhones and some Tesla parts, both linked to Tata clients. Documents linked to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (2330.TW) and Qualcomm (QCOM.O), both key Apple suppliers, were also part of the leak.New documents reviewed by Reuters show that at least six files map parts of the iPhone 18 Pro models to specific suppliers. These include parts for the main circuit board, battery and camera systems.A source familiar with the matter said Apple treats this supplier information as highly sensitive, especially for unreleased products. The documents reportedly show supplier details that Apple does not publish publicly.Overall, the files cover hundreds of parts expected to be used in the iPhone 18 Pro models. They also show where Apple uses multiple suppliers for some parts and fewer suppliers for others, revealing both strengths and risks in its supply chain.World Leaks has previously claimed responsibility for a breach involving Nike. Reuters has not confirmed the authenticity of the data and could not reach the group for comment. AppleInsider first reported that iPhone 18 Pro documents were included in the Tata leak.Reuters has reported that Apple is investigating the breach and working with Tata on security steps. Tata has restricted access to internal systems and hired a global consultancy for a forensic audit.Some leaked files also carry Apple “confidential” marks and internal code names linked to the iPhone 18 Pro series, according to a source.The leak folder also includes photos of iPhones being drop-tested at a Tata facility in early 2026. The images show a grey phone with a triple rear camera setup and the Apple logo. The source said these are iPhone 18 Pro models, although Reuters could not confirm this.India is expected to make 26% of global iPhone production in 2026, up from 6% four years ago, according to Counterpoint Research.
Article Summary: The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack. The exposure could impact Apple's tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones.

Article Title: Researchers just found that job seekers may be better off replying fast, because an analysis of 11.6 million marketplace interactions suggests slower responses hurt hiring chances and quick replies ma
Article Text:
In today's job market, especially for freelancers, how quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Speed Is a Signal: When

Faster Replies

Increase Hiring Likelihood

The hidden psychology of instant

digital communication

New research reveals that employers form quick impressions based on reply times, often interpreting faster responses as enthusiasm and reliability. This study highlights that even small delays can cost you opportunities, making prompt communication a crucial skill for professional success in the modern workplace. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Getting an undeniable advantage in the contemporary hybrid workplace

When we look at the small anxieties built into modern technology, few things are as tense as the text bubble. We watch those three little dots dance on our screens, or we glance at our phones, hoping for a notification banner to break a long, quiet afternoon silence. In personal life, people often read response times as a sign of anything from busyness to distance. For a long time, we treated these digital habits as simple, casual quirks of our social lives. But new tracking data shows that even small delays can matter when you are trying to land a job.Imagine submitting a spectacular portfolio or matching perfectly with a freelance listing online, only to have the entire opportunity slip away simply because you decided to wait until after dinner to reply to an initial inquiry. In the modern, always-connected freelance market, your professional destiny is often decided long before you ever jump on an introductory phone call or sit down for a formal video panel. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply.This profound behavioural pattern was detailed in a research paper published in the journal Management Science titled. The study utilised transaction data from approximately eleven point six million real-world marketplace interactions on a massive global freelancing portal.The data showed that longer reply delays were linked to a lower chance of getting the contract, making response speed an important competitive factor.To comprehend how the quick message influences contemporary recruitment managers, one should consider the psychological factors that exist beyond the screen. When the client contacts the freelancer or a potential worker, they do not only assess their technical competence, past projects, or star rating. What they need is to find the intangible level of professional security and trust between both parties. According to the results obtained during several experiment stages, a quick answer could be interpreted as a sign of enthusiasm and hard work.The behavioural trials suggest that replying quickly can change how an evaluator judges a candidate's character. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts. It also signalled future responsiveness, making employers more likely to see the candidate as organised and reliable.This study reveals the possibility that the halo effect could operate regardless of the message content or past quality metrics. Sometimes, even if the slow responder received better reviews or had a lower price, he still managed to win the contract. Reading a late message is likely to be taken as a signal of indifference or lack of organisation, which makes it harder to contact the slower employee under deadlines.The practical insights gained from this large-scale market research data provide the key tool for all those who are working in the competitive world of freelance and remote recruitment. In the world of hybrid workforces and teams around the globe, being reachable becomes a valuable professional skill that can be used to enhance one’s market value without any additional certification.By setting up notifications, keeping templates ready, and replying promptly during hiring windows, independent professionals can signal professionalism to potential buyers. This proactive approach helps build an immediate sense of social comfort and trust with a potential client before any physical work even begins. It can turn a digital exchange into a more personal interaction and give fast responders an advantage over slower competitors.The results of the research suggest that reaction time could be regarded as a signal. Fast reactions of the respondents will help to reassure the client, establish one’s reputation and increase one’s opportunities of being hired. Workers may demonstrate their professionalism by fast reactions.
Article Summary: How quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts.

Article Title: As riding costs hurt, Tesla tells employees: You cannot spend more than $200 on…
Article Text:
From ‘use more AI’ to ‘please, use less’



The carve-out that points straight to xAI



Why the bill matters more than usual for Tesla



Tesla has told its staff to stop burning cash on artificial intelligence. Starting July 6, employees can spend no more than $200 a week on AI tools, according to an internal memo first reported by The Information. The cap arrives just months after Tesla was pushing workers to use AI as hard as they could—a whiplash turn that says a lot about where the AI spending boom is heading.Anyone who wants to go past the $200 line now needs sign-off. Before the limit, some software engineers were reportedly chewing through thousands of dollars in tokens every week. Tokens are the units that measure AI usage, and every prompt eats into the bill.The reversal is fast even by Tesla standards. Over the past six months, the company moved scattered AI use onto a single approved platform called Bottle Rocket, stocked with models from OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Cursor. Some teams built dashboards that ranked employees by how many tokens they consumed, nudging everyone to use more.It worked too well. The heaviest users ran up weekly bills in the thousands, and now the same company that gamified consumption is slamming on the brakes.Here's the detail that gives the game away. The $200 cap does not apply to beta versions of xAI products—Elon Musk's own AI company. So the policy quietly steers heavy users toward Grok and Cursor's Composer model rather than rivals.Musk has spent months nudging Tesla staff toward tools tied to his other ventures. He emailed the whole company urging people to try Composer after his AI lab started working with Cursor. SpaceX is now set to buy Cursor's parent, Anysphere, for $60 billion.The push isn't landing. Grok is unpopular inside Tesla, and many engineers reach for Anthropic's Claude instead, according to people familiar with the matter. When you need a spending limit to win internal users for your own product, that's not a vote of confidence.Tesla's whole valuation now rests on AI. Musk has said the company's future depends on deploying it across the Robotaxi network and the Optimus humanoid robot, not on selling cars—and revenue has been roughly flat for two years. That makes the spending story awkward. If Tesla can't manage a few thousand dollars of weekly token spend per engineer, questions about scaling AI across a robotaxi fleet and millions of robots are fair game.The move mirrors a wider corporate retreat from "tokenmaxxing," the trend of measuring AI productivity by sheer usage. Uber capped spending at $1,500 a month after blowing through its entire 2026 AI budget by April. Meta, Amazon, and Walmart have all rolled out caps or steered staff toward cheaper models as token-based billing exposed them to the true cost of every prompt.Tesla has been tightening on security too, restricting access to models outside Bottle Rocket on company machines and warning staff not to feed confidential data into unapproved systems. For now, the message to Tesla's engineers is simple: the free-spending phase is over, and the meter is running.
Article Summary: Tesla has told its staff to stop burning cash on artificial intelligence. Starting July 6, employees can spend no more than $200 a week on AI tools. The cap arrives just months after Tesla was pushing workers to use AI as hard as they could. The heaviest users ran up weekly bills in the thousands.

Article Title: Xbox has a plan for your disc collection, just as Sony kills physical games
Article Text:
As Sony ditches discs, Xbox tests a way to digitize your games

The feature works the way you'd want it to, mostly

Older games don't make the cut

This is really about the next Xbox

Buy a game, own a game—that used to be the deal. Sony changed it this week, announcing the end of PlayStation disc production after 2028. But the same day, Xbox quietly moved in a different direction, and it might matter more to anyone with a shelf full of cases. Microsoft is testing a feature called "Disc2Digital" that converts your physical games into digital copies you can play without the disc. The Verge reported the details, and what they add up to is clear. The all-digital future is coming fast, but Xbox doesn't want collectors left holding dead plastic.Slot a disc into your Xbox, install the game, and play it while signed into your Microsoft account. That's it. The console reads the disc and hands you a digital copy. No codes, no proof of purchase. References to "enable Disc2Digital" first showed up in the Xbox PC app code in May, and The Verge reports internal testing kicked off soon after.The catch sits in the fine print. Your digital copy is chained to the disc, not your account. Sell the game or lend it to a friend, and the digital version jumps to whoever loads the disc next. Think of it as a physical game wearing a digital coat—you get discless installs, plus cloud streaming and Play Anywhere access across PCs and handhelds when a title supports them.Not everything qualifies. Disc2Digital covers Xbox One and Xbox Series X/S discs only. Original Xbox and Xbox 360 titles are locked out, so those classics stay on the disc or nowhere. Even some Xbox One games might miss the cut—Microsoft told testers that eligibility hinges on how and when a disc was manufactured, and older pressings may lack the data the feature needs.The concept isn't new. Microsoft floated nearly the same idea for the Xbox One back in 2013, then buried it after fans revolted over always-online rules. What's changed is the ground beneath it. Roughly four in five PlayStation games already sell digitally—the market that once rejected this now runs on it.Follow the thread and it leads to Project Helix, Microsoft's next-gen console. The Verge says Microsoft hasn't decided whether it ships with a disc drive, though Windows Central reports the company is leaning toward cutting it—the same road Sony looks set to take with the PS6.If Helix goes disc-free, Disc2Digital stops being a bonus and becomes the bridge. It's the difference between carrying your collection to the new hardware and starting over. Microsoft is running internal tests now, with more expected in the coming months. Whether that's a genuine olive branch to physical fans or just a softer landing into a digital-only world depends on how the final rules shake out.
Article Summary: Microsoft is testing a feature called "Disc2Digital" that converts your physical games into digital copies. The feature works the way you'd want it to, mostly, but older games don't make the cut. Original Xbox and Xbox 360 titles are locked out, so those classics stay on the disc or nowhere.

Article Title: Quote of the Day by Accenture CEO Julie Sweet: 'You can't get the growth without the skills'
Article Text:
Julie Sweet, CEO, Accenture

Quote of the day by Julie Sweet



What Julie Sweet's quote means



A message reflected in her work



A simple takeaway



Julie Sweet has never been someone who reaches for comfortable abstractions when a direct statement will do. The Chair and CEO of Accenture, one of the world's largest professional services companies with over 700,000 employees across more than 120 countries, has built her leadership reputation on clarity of thought and an unflinching focus on what actually drives results. This quote is a precise example of that quality. It does not gesture toward the importance of learning or suggest that skills are a nice complement to ambition. It states a dependency. Growth without skills is not slower growth. It is not growth at all."You can't get the growth without the skills."The quote is structured as a conditional, and the conditional is absolute. Sweet is not saying skills help growth, or that skilled people tend to grow faster. She is saying the relationship is non-negotiable. You cannot have one without the other. That framing matters because it removes the mental escape hatch that most individuals and organisations quietly rely on, the idea that momentum, vision, or opportunity can carry you forward even when capability has not kept pace.The word "get" is doing more work than it appears to. Growth in Sweet's vocabulary is not passive, it is not something that happens to you if conditions are favourable.You get growth. You go after it, build toward it, and earn it. And the thing that makes you capable of getting it is skills, specifically the right skills for the moment you are in, not the skills that worked a cycle ago.There is also a directional logic embedded in the quote that is easy to miss. Most people think about skills as preparation for growth, something you build in advance of opportunity arriving. Sweet's framing suggests the relationship runs in both directions. Skills do not just enable growth. The pursuit of growth reveals which skills you are missing and forces you to develop them. The two things are not sequential. They are interdependent, each pulling the other forward.For organisations, this has significant strategic implications. A company can set ambitious revenue targets, enter new markets, and acquire competitors, but if the workforce does not have the capabilities to execute in those new contexts, the growth will not stick. Sweet has made this argument publicly in the context of artificial intelligence, arguing repeatedly that the companies falling behind on AI adoption are not failing because of a technology problem. They are failing because of a skills problem.Few CEOs have put skills development as close to the centre of their strategic agenda as Sweet has at Accenture. Under her leadership, the company has invested over a billion dollars annually in training and reskilling its workforce, an extraordinary figure that reflects a genuine institutional belief that human capability is not a fixed resource to be managed but a dynamic one to be continuously developed.Sweet has spoken publicly about the challenge of the AI transition specifically, noting that the speed at which AI is reshaping professional work means that skills built even two or three years ago may already be insufficient for what the next wave of client demands will require. Her response has not been to slow the adoption of AI but to accelerate the investment in the people who need to work alongside it.She has also been candid that this is not purely altruistic. Accenture's business model depends on delivering expertise that clients cannot easily replicate internally. If the skills of its people plateau, so does the value it can deliver, and so does its ability to grow. The quote is, in that sense, a description of Accenture's own operating logic as much as a piece of general wisdom.The quote lands differently depending on where you are in your career. For someone early in their professional life, it is a reminder that ambition without investment in capability is optimism without a plan. For someone mid-career, it is a useful diagnostic: if growth has stalled, the question worth asking is not what opportunities are missing but what skills are. For leaders and organisations, it is a strategic principle with a clear implication. Skills are not a cost centre sitting alongside the growth strategy. They are the growth strategy. Sweet's career, and the company she runs, are the proof of concept.
Article Summary: Julie Sweet is CEO of Accenture, one of the world's largest professional services companies. The quote is structured as a conditional, and the conditional is absolute. Sweet is not saying skills help growth, but that the relationship is non-negotiable.

Article Title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang 'plans' to join Elon Musk and Sundar Pichai in space as the world's most valuable company is quietly adding to the team behind ...
Article Text:
"System Software Principal Architect -

Orbital Data Center

"

"Orbital Data Center System Architect,"

Read Nvidia’s space data centre job post description as on LinkedIn



"Space-1 is NVIDIA's first Orbital Data Center (ODC) module — a Vera Rubin–class compute platform engineered for low-Earth orbit mission. It is the first step in a multi-generation orbital roadmap to speed up AI adoption. We are looking for a strong technical architect to own end-to-end system software architecture for Space-1 and successor orbital platforms. You will architect the full stack — application to libraries, from data center stack to BMC and BIOS firmware, manageability, and telemetry through the host OS, GPU and CPU drivers, and CUDA — to deliver a production-ready inference platform that operates reliably in the radiation, thermal-cycling, and remote-operations environment of LEO. You will partner closely with the orbital hardware system architecture team, drive customer use cases with constellation operators, align architecture with mission requirements, and bring the best orbital AI products to market. Join us at the forefront of technological advancement.”

Nvidia appears to be expanding its efforts in space computing. The world's most valuable company has recently posted a job opening for its Space-1 project, its first computing system designed for space missions. The latest hiring move comes months after the company unveiled Space-1 at its GTC conference and suggests it is continuing work on software for orbital AI infrastructure. The new opening is for aa role that will help develop the software powering Space-1. Earlier this year, Nvidia also advertised for ana position focused on designing the overall architecture of an orbital data centre.Several technology companies have also shown interest in space-based computing and connectivity. Elon Musk 's SpaceX is among the companies working on technologies for space infrastructure, while Google parent Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has previously highlighted the role of satellite connectivity through Google services. A Wall Street Journal report from May also claimed that Google is in talks with Elon Musk-owned SpaceX for a rocket-launch deal to put orbital data centres in space.The latest role will focus on developing software capable of operating in the harsh conditions of space, including radiation exposure and extreme temperature fluctuations. The software will also be designed for remote management. The position requires prior experience in developing AI systems and space-based computing infrastructure. Nvidia has listed a base salary range of $272,000 to $431,250, excluding equity compensation and other benefits. This comes after the company's CEO Jensen Huang said that the economics of space computing are currently challenging but are expected to improve over time during Nvidia's recent earnings call.The company wrote,The company's latest hiring activity comes as interest grows in orbital data centers, which are being explored as a way to address increasing constraints related to land, power and cooling for AI infrastructure on Earth.
Article Summary: Nvidia has posted a job opening for its Space-1 project, its first computing system designed for space missions. The new role will focus on developing software capable of operating in the harsh conditions of space, including radiation exposure and extreme temperature fluctuations. The position requires prior experience in developing AI systems and space-based computing.

Amazon's Prime Day 2026 sale goes live at midnight on July 4 and runs for 72 hours, until 11:59 pm on July 6. This marks the tenth edition of the event in India. Early deals are already live for Prime members. New annual memberships are priced at Rs 999, down from Rs 1,499, with a Shopping Edition at Rs 299 a year and Prime Lite at Rs 599. Additional savings include a 10% instant discount on SBI and Axis Bank cards, 5% unlimited cashback on the Amazon Pay ICICI credit card, and No Cost EMI for up to 12 months on select products. Here are the best deals on consumer tech this Prime Day, across smartphones, audio, wearables, smart home devices and screens.The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra 5G is priced at Rs 84,999, down from its Rs 1,29,999 launch price. It has a 200MP main camera, the Galaxy AI suite, a bundled S Pen and an all-day battery. Exchange offers worth up to Rs 66,000 apply on top, along with No Cost EMI for up to 12 months.The Samsung Galaxy S25 FE is priced at Rs 44,999, with an additional 5% cashback. Amazon is calling this the lowest price the phone has seen anywhere. It has a 120Hz Dynamic AMOLED 2X display, a 50MP triple camera system and the same Galaxy AI toolkit as the Ultra, without the S Pen or telephoto zoom.The iQoo Z11x is priced at Rs 19,999, with a Dimensity 7400-Turbo chipset, a 7,200mAh battery and a 50MP Sony camera with 4K recording on both the front and rear units. The iQoo Neo 10 starts at Rs 38,999, with a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset, iQoo's Q1 co-processor and vapour-chamber cooling.The iQoo 15R starts at Rs 45,999, with a 7,600mAh battery in a 7. 9mm frame and a Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 chip. The iQoo 15 is available from Rs 71,999, including a coupon offer, with a 2K LTPO AMOLED display peaking at 2,600 nits, a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor and 100W wired charging.The Redmi 15 5G, with a Snapdragon 6s Gen 3 chipset and a 7,000mAh battery, is priced at Rs 24,498, down from its Rs 36,999 launch price. The Samsung Galaxy A17 5G (8GB+128GB) is priced at Rs 24,499, down from Rs 29,999, with a 6.7-inch display, a 50MP main camera and a 5,000mAh battery. The Redmi A7 Pro 5G starts at Rs 12,999. The Realme Narzo 100 Lite 5G is priced at Rs 16,249, with a 7,000mAh battery, and the Tecno POP X 5G is priced at Rs 16,999.The Sennheiser Momentum True Wireless 4 in Black Copper is down 40% to Rs 17,990, with 24-bit/96kHz TrueResponse drivers and a six-mic call array. The Momentum 4 Wireless Special Edition, with 42mm transducers, adaptive noise cancellation and a 60-hour battery, is priced at Rs 21,990. The HD 490 PRO Plus, an open-back reference headphone for studio use, is priced at Rs 27,999. The Profile Wireless clip-on mic kits are priced at Rs 14,990 for the single-channel set and Rs 20,990 for the two-channel set, and the Profile USB Microphone starts at Rs 6,990.Oura is discounting the Ring 4 smart ring by up to 30%, its largest India price cut since launch. The Ring 4 tracks sleep, readiness and activity without a screen. No Cost EMI over nine months brings the price down to roughly Rs 2,248 a month during the sale.The BenQ MW560C business projector is discounted by up to 41%, the MX560C by up to 40% and the TH575i home projector by up to 37%. Among monitors, the GW2790Q is down by up to 37% and the GW2490C by up to 35%, while the portable GV50 projector is down by up to 34%. The ScreenBar Halo 2 monitor light is discounted by up to 32%. BenQ's MA series for Mac users, RD series programming monitors, PD series designer monitors and MOBIUZ gaming monitors are also discounted through the sale.The Dreame L50s Pro Ultra robot vacuum is priced at Rs 74,999, down from Rs 79,999, and the L50 Ultra CE is priced at Rs 59,999, down from Rs 64,999. Among stick vacuums, the Mova J10 is priced at Rs 5,999, down from Rs 6,999, and the K10 Pro is priced at Rs 18,999. Purchases carry a one-year warranty and access to Dreame's service network across more than 160 Indian cities.The Lava Agni 4 (8GB+256GB) works out to a net effective Rs 24,249 after SBI and Axis Bank card offers, against a sticker price of Rs 27,999. The Bold N2 5G comes down to a net Rs 11,249, and the Agni 3 (8GB+256GB) is available for a net Rs 20,249. These offers began on July 2, two days before the main sale window.The Sony BRAVIA 3 Series, a 4K Google TV with Dolby Atmos and Sony's X1 processor, is priced at Rs 62,990. The Samsung Vision AI Smart Mini LED TV is priced at Rs 38,990, its lowest price yet. The TCL Smart QD-Mini LED Google TV is priced at Rs 40,990, with Dolby Vision and 30W Dolby Audio, and the Xiaomi Smart Google LED TV is priced at Rs 19,999 with Dolby Vision support.The HP Victus Gaming Laptop, with a Ryzen 7 processor and an RTX 4050 GPU, is priced at Rs 78,990, inclusive of bank discounts. The Xiaomi Pad 8, with a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset and an 11.2-inch 3. 2K display, is priced at Rs 29,999.Amazon Pay ICICI Bank credit card holders earn unlimited 5% cashback on every Amazon. in purchase through Prime Day, on top of the listed discount. New cardholders also get a complimentary year of Prime. SBI and Axis Bank credit and debit cards unlock a further 10% instant discount on payments and EMI transactions. Carts above Rs 1,500 qualify for Amazon's Pay-in-3 No Cost EMI, which splits the bill interest-free across three months. These offers do not apply automatically and have to be selected at checkout.The Prime Day 2026 sale runs from July 4 to July 6 on Amazon. in.
Article Summary: Amazon's Prime Day 2026 sale runs July 4-6, offering discounts across smartphones, audio, wearables and more. The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra drops to Rs 84,999, while the Sennheiser MOMENTUM TWS 4 is priced at Rs 17,990. Other deals include the iQOO 15, Oura Ring 4 at its biggest India discount, BenQ projectors and monitors, Dreame vacuums, and TVs from Sony and Samsung.
